title: fno a2a status-breakpoint event (protocol family v1)
description: One events.jsonl line for the four task-boundary event types (task_started, task_done, blocked, run_summary). Self-contained (no internal references) so third-party observers validate against it without running fno. Routable fields live at envelope level; only type-specific payload stays in data. Stability: additive-only within v:1; a breaking change bumps v.

v.description: Wire version of the protocol family. Additive-only within v:1; a breaking change bumps this.

source.description: Producer identity; consumers route on this. A fixed-string source, or a per-agent worker.

from.description: Sender canonical handle (harness:session). Present on session producers only; a non-session producer (cron, bare shell) omits it entirely.

model.description: Sender's own model string, or 'unknown'. Present on session producers only; omitted otherwise.

node.description: Backlog node id the work is for. Example values use placeholder ids (e.g. prj-0001).

task.description: Task id within the plan. Omitted for a flat quick-plan with no task ids.

run.description: Target-run id (the target-minted session id). The dedup identity: consumers dedup on (run, task, type). Distinct from the harness session id.

parent.description: Spawn-lineage handle of the parent, when this run was spawned by another agent.

outcome.description: Return-contract outcome. Present only on task_done and run_summary.

data.description: Per-type payload; see the protocol spec for per-type fields.
